title: Hieracium leucurolepium
taxonomy: Hieracium leucurolepium is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Hieracium, which comprises over 300 species of hawkweeds. Further, it is divided into the subgenus Pilosella, which is a distinct group within the Hieracium genus. The species Hieracium leucurolepium is native to Europe, specifically in the mountainous regions. The exact classification is further supported by its characteristics and morphological features.
uses: Hieracium leucurolepium, also known as the yellow hawkweed, is a plant that has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. However, there is limited scientific research on its specific medicinal uses. Some studies suggest that Hieracium leucurolepium extracts may have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which could be beneficial for wound healing and other health applications. More research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of this plant. In some traditional medicine practices, Hieracium leucurolepium is used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues.

intro: Hieracium leucurolepium, also known as mountain hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that grows in mountainous regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium leucurolepium include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
